cognates.correspondence_path: Ar ث ↔ He שׁ — regular *ṯ reflex
Ar ث ↔ Aram ת — regular *ṯ reflex; note the secondary r-insertion/dissimilation seen in Aramaic/Ugaritic vs Central Semitic
cognates.notes: Aramaic often exhibits an r-insertion or metathesis pattern common in numerals for 'two' in this branch (cf. Ugaritic ṯn).

cognates.correspondence_path: Ar ث ↔ Ug ṯ — regular *ṯ reflex
cognates.notes: Ugaritic maintains the root without the final yod suffix observed in Arabic or Hebrew dual forms.

caveats: v0 prototype: this is an LLM-generated analysis, not a lexicon lookup. Results may be incomplete or incorrect, especially for Syriac, Tigrinya, and rare forms. Verify against Wiktionary or a scholarly lexicon before citing.
The root ث-ن-ي is treated here as a III-y base because of the yod in the dual/nominal morphology of Arabic and Hebrew.
